page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter Ironman 2026: Recovery Cruises & Spectator Views

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella yacht charter for Ironman 2026 — recovery cruises with onboard drinks, spectator views of the swim & bike course from Puerto Banús. From €749/2h.
data.summary: Our Marbella yacht charter for Ironman 2026 offers recovery cruises and spectator views from Puerto Banús. Relax with onboard drinks and snacks while watching the swim and bike course, or celebrate your finish aboard a private boat.
key_facts: Ironman 2026 Marbella: swim, bike, and run along the Costa del Sol.
Spectator cruises depart Puerto Banús — watch the swim start and bike route.
Recovery charters: post-race relaxation with drinks, snacks, and a licensed skipper.
Fleet from 6.4m speedboats to 29m luxury yachts, all with IVA included.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749/2h, all-inclusive.
Tier B Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) includes a free jet ski from €4,719/4h.
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.
Book via WhatsApp for custom durations and boat availability.

faq.a: The exact date for Ironman Marbella 2026 is typically in late April or early May. The event includes a 3.8 km swim in the Mediterranean, a 180 km bike course through the hills, and a 42.2 km run along the coast. Check the official Ironman calendar for confirmation.

faq.q: Can I watch the Ironman swim from a yacht?
faq.a: Yes. Our boats depart from Puerto Banús, close to the swim start area. From the deck, you get a clear view of the athletes entering the water. The Mangusta 80 and Azimut 58 Flybridge offer spacious flybridges for panoramic viewing.

faq.q: What is included in a recovery cruise after Ironman?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and Spanish VAT. You just relax and enjoy the sea breeze. The Astondoa 40 is a popular choice for small groups celebrating a finish.

faq.q: How much does a yacht charter cost for Ironman 2026?
faq.a: Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours, all inclusive. Tier B Mangusta 80 starts at €4,719 for 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Which boat is best for a group of 12 spectators?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), or Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax) all accommodate 12 guests. The Ferretti 94 (29m) is ideal if you want extra space for a post-race party.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food or decorations for a celebration?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring additional food, cakes, or decorations. Our standard charter includes light snacks and drinks, but you can arrange catering or a private chef via WhatsApp. Let us know in advance.

faq.q: Do you offer pick-up from Marbella Marina or other locations?
faq.a: All our boats depart from Puerto Banús. If you are staying on the Golden Mile or near Marbella town, we can arrange a taxi or water taxi transfer. Contact us via WhatsApp for bespoke logistics.

faq.q: What is the cancellation policy for Ironman charters?
faq.a: Cancellations made 14 days or more before the charter receive a full refund. Within 7–13 days, 50% refund. Less than 7 days, no refund unless we can rebook the boat. Weather-related cancellations are handled case by case.

data.body_html:
Ironman Marbella 2026 is one of the most demanding endurance events on the Costa del Sol. Whether you are an athlete recovering after the finish line or a supporter wanting the best view of the swim and bike course, a Marbella yacht charter Ironman 2026 offers a unique way to experience the day. Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús, minutes from the swim start and the coastal bike route. From €749 for 2 hours, all inclusive of skipper, drinks, snacks, and VAT, you can relax on the water while the action unfolds around you.

Why Choose a Yacht for Ironman 2026?

The Ironman course in Marbella takes athletes from the beach at Puerto Banús into the Mediterranean for a 3.8 km swim, then through the hills of Estepona and Benahavís on the bike leg, and finally along the promenade to the finish near the Golden Mile. Watching from a private boat gives you an unobstructed view of the swim start and the first few kilometres of the bike route. After the race, a recovery cruise lets you soothe tired muscles with a cool drink and the sea breeze. Our boat rental Marbella service makes it simple—just book, board, and enjoy.

Best Boats for Spectator Views

For large groups, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers a spacious flybridge and sundeck, perfect for scanning the water. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) is another excellent choice, with panoramic windows and a shaded aft deck. If you prefer a more intimate experience, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are nimble and comfortable. All depart from Puerto Banús, so you can be on the water within 10 minutes of booking.

Recovery Cruises: What’s Included?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. Insurance and Spanish IVA (21%) are always included—no hidden fees. For athletes, this means you can focus on rehydration and relaxation. The Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) is a popular choice for small groups of finishers, with a fast planing hull that can cruise to Cala del Faro for a swim stop. Book via WhatsApp to confirm availability for your preferred time.

Pricing Overview for Ironman 2026

BoatCapacityPrice (2h)Price (8h)
Astondoa 409 pax€749€2,299
Azimut 3911 pax€749€2,299
Mangusta 80 (all variants)12 pax€4,719 (4h min)On request
Other boatsVariesPricing via WhatsApp

All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT. The Mangusta 80 also includes a free jet ski for your charter.

Where to Watch the Ironman from the Water

The swim start is directly off the beach at Puerto Banús. Our boats can anchor just outside the buoyed course, giving you a front-row seat. As athletes transition to the bike, you can cruise along the coast toward Estepona or Cabopino, depending on the route. The bike course typically heads inland, but you can return to the marina to catch the run finish. For the best all-round view, the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) offers a raised flybridge with 360-degree sightlines. Contact us via WhatsApp to discuss the best itinerary for your group.

Book Your Marbella Yacht Charter Ironman 2026

Spaces fill quickly during event weekends. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance. All charters are confirmed via WhatsApp, and we can tailor the duration to your schedule—whether you want a 2-hour spectator cruise or a full-day recovery trip to Cala Cortés. Our Marbella yacht charter Ironman 2026 page has all the details, but for real-time availability, message us directly. We look forward to welcoming you aboard.

Frequently Asked Questions

When is Ironman Marbella 2026?

The exact date for Ironman Marbella 2026 is typically in late April or early May. The event includes a 3.8 km swim in the Mediterranean, a 180 km bike course through the hills, and a 42.2 km run along the coast. Check the official Ironman calendar for confirmation.

Can I watch the Ironman swim from a yacht?

Yes. Our boats depart from Puerto Banús, close to the swim start area. From the deck, you get a clear view of the athletes entering the water. The Mangusta 80 and Azimut 58 Flybridge offer spacious flybridges for panoramic viewing.

What is included in a recovery cruise after Ironman?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and Spanish VAT. You just relax and enjoy the sea breeze. The Astondoa 40 is a popular choice for small groups celebrating a finish.

How much does a yacht charter cost for Ironman 2026?

Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours, all inclusive. Tier B Mangusta 80 starts at €4,719 for 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

Which boat is best for a group of 12 spectators?

The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), or Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax) all accommodate 12 guests. The Ferretti 94 (29m) is ideal if you want extra space for a post-race party.

Can I bring my own food or decorations for a celebration?

Yes, you are welcome to bring additional food, cakes, or decorations. Our standard charter includes light snacks and drinks, but you can arrange catering or a private chef via WhatsApp. Let us know in advance.

Do you offer pick-up from Marbella Marina or other locations?

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús. If you are staying on the Golden Mile or near Marbella town, we can arrange a taxi or water taxi transfer. Contact us via WhatsApp for bespoke logistics.

What is the cancellation policy for Ironman charters?

Cancellations made 14 days or more before the charter receive a full refund. Within 7–13 days, 50% refund. Less than 7 days, no refund unless we can rebook the boat. Weather-related cancellations are handled case by case.
